# AI Instructions

## Workflow

- Complete only the requested task.
- Never continue to another milestone unless asked.
- Never redesign existing code unless requested.
- Never overwrite user changes.
- Never create placeholder content.
- Stop after completing the requested work.
- Always summarize:
  - Files changed
  - What changed
  - Any manual steps required

## Design

Follow the Lure Encyclopedia Design System.

The visual language is:

- Editorial
- Museum-quality
- Minimal
- Timeless
- Large whitespace
- Serif headings
- Sans-serif body text
- Hairline borders
- No drop shadows
- No rounded corners unless already defined
- Photography first

## Development

When adding code:

- Reuse existing components.
- Keep CSS modular.
- Prefer semantic HTML.
- Do not introduce unnecessary JavaScript.
- Do not install packages unless requested.
- Keep templates Ghost-compatible.

## Before editing

Always inspect the existing file first.

Modify instead of replacing whenever possible.

## After editing

Provide:

- Files modified
- Why each change was made
- Suggested next step

Then stop.---

# Homepage Design Authority

The file:

docs/design/homepage-v2.png

is the authoritative visual reference for the homepage.

When implementing homepage components:

- Match the approved mockup as closely as possible.
- Prefer the mockup over previous implementations.
- Do not invent layouts.
- Do not redesign components.
- Preserve editorial spacing and typography.
- Use existing design tokens whenever possible.

---

# Implementation Workflow

Implement exactly one component per request.

Complete only the requested component.

After each implementation:

1. Summarize files changed.
2. Explain why they changed.
3. Stop and wait for approval.

Never continue to additional homepage sections unless explicitly instructed.
